method_atom: A computational scheme for testing whether a proposed continuous geometric symmetry of a field theory survives beyond the weak-coupling (perturbative) regime: the continuous domain is discretized onto a finite grid, field configurations are sampled by stochastic (Monte Carlo) simulation at finite coupling, and closed-contour functionals that the symmetry predicts to be equal (here, functionals of equal enclosed measure but different contour shapes) are compared. Systematic discrepancies among such symmetry-related observables quantify the degree of symmetry breaking and reveal which residual subgroup, if any, persists.
object_atom: U(1) Yang-Mills gauge theory formulated on a non-commutative plane, and the fate of its invariance under area-preserving diffeomorphisms — probed via Wilson loops of the same enclosed area but different shapes — including whether the rigid SL(2,R) subgroup survives at finite gauge coupling and finite non-commutativity.
brief_reason: The lattice-plus-Monte-Carlo strategy of comparing observables related by a claimed symmetry to measure how far that symmetry survives non-perturbatively is field-neutral and transferable, while the non-commutative U(1) gauge theory with area-preserving-diffeomorphism/SL(2,R) invariance and Wilson-loop observables is the physics-specific object.
